publishedAbstract: There may be instances where there are issues with the rook-ceph-mon pod resource. Quorum on the backend (Ceph) may not be attained. An example of this is when more than one host exists, the rook-ceph-operator will always attempt to maintain High Availability (HA). This means that when a rook-ceph-mon pod is down for an extended period of time, the rook-ceph-operator will create a new rook-ceph-mon deployment. However, in the event the previous rook-ceph-mon was not removed, there may be an issue where there are four rook-ceph-mon deployments on the frontend (ODF), and three monitor daemons on the backend (Ceph), resulting in degraded quorum.
publishedTitle: How to Remove an Unwanted Ceph Monitor (rook-ceph-mon) from ODF
